items.title: Security update available for Adobe Commerce
items.summary: This update resolves critical, important, and moderate vulnerabilities in Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source, including issues that could lead to arbitrary code execution, security feature bypass, and privilege escalation. Eight vulnerabilities are rated Critical, with the highest CVSS score of 9.6.

items.title: Amasty patches 25 Magento extensions, 1 critical (RCE)
items.summary: Sansec reported that Amasty issued a massive security release for 25 extensions, with one critical vulnerability that can lead to remote code execution. Sansec Shield already blocks the critical issues.

items.title: Unauthenticated file upload in Amasty Order Attributes for Magento
items.summary: An unauthenticated arbitrary file upload flaw in Amasty Order Attributes allows attackers to write executable files to a Magento store without logging in, potentially leading to remote code execution.

items.title: Critical vulnerability in Mirasvit Cache Warmer for Magento
items.summary: Sansec discovered an unauthenticated PHP object injection vulnerability in Mirasvit Cache Warmer, a full-page cache extension, which can lead to remote code execution with a CVSS score of 9.8. Mirasvit released a patched version (1.11.12) on May 25, 2026.

items.title: Adobe Commerce DOS Vulnerability
items.summary: Adobe published fixed releases addressing CVE-2026-34654, an Improper Input Validation vulnerability that could lead to application denial-of-service. Administrators should upgrade to the fixed Commerce version.

items.title: PolyShell: unrestricted file upload in Magento and Adobe Commerce
items.summary: Sansec discovered a critical flaw, named PolyShell, in Magento's REST API that allows unauthenticated attackers to upload executable files to any store by using a polyglot to bypass validation. Attacks have been observed on a significant percentage of stores.

items.title: Critical Security Update: Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source
items.summary: This critical security update addresses multiple vulnerabilities in Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source, including issues that could allow attackers to bypass security controls, gain escalated privileges, and execute unauthorized malicious code. The patch fixes several high-risk entry points, including incorrect authorization (RCE), improper access control (data leak), stored XSS (admin hijacking), and path traversal (file exposure).
